Hymns and songs include In Christ Alone, Standing in the Need of Prayer and When I Survey the Wondrous Cross, sung by Faye Simpson, Katie Ritson and the Adventist Vocal Ensemble

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Rosins sample life in Brisbane for a week, but while Julia's dream is to move to Australia in the hope of a better lifestyle for their children, husband Hussein is not so sure

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young help two teams challenged to scour an antiques fair to pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Charlie Ross hosts, and also pays a visit to the Norfolk Tank Museum

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits L'Abri fellowship in Hampshire to explore Christian faith and hospitality. Plus, music from Laura Wright, who performs with the Choir of Royal Holloway

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the men's singles final at Melbourne Park in Australia, which was won by Novak Djokovic for a record-extending eighth time last year

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers head to in Trowbridge, Wiltshire, to help Jacqui and Yung, who have never had time to fix up the tired garden of their new home

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap explores Northamptonshire's Nene Valley, finding out how a farmer who was fighting flooding is now embracing wetland wildlife to make his land pay. Adam Henson hopes for good news from his ewes as he discovers how many are in lamb, while Charlotte Smith investigates whether Brexit is delivering all it promised for the UK's fishing industry and ecologist Josh Styles reveals the secret world inside a prehistoric plant

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Windermere Jetty Museum in the Lake District, where items assessed include a collection of silver trophies awarded to a pioneering female sailor. There is also a James Bond poster scraped off a wall, a guitar rescued from a skip, a wooden dingy from the film Swallows & Amazons, and a pair of necklaces with surprising values

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: While investigating when a car belonging to a former IRA member is pulled from Strangford Lough, DCI Tom Brannick recognises the calling card of a serial killer from 20 years ago known as Goliath - one of whose victims was the detective's wife. Despite opposition from old friend DCS Jackie Twomey, Brannick and partner DS Niamh McGovern break open the cold case in the hope that it will help them. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt, with Charlene McKenna and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Arsenal v Manchester City at Etihad Stadium, Aston Villa v Leicester City at Villa Park, West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium and Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Olly Alexander: Growing Up Gay
programme.desc: The Years & Years lead singer explores why members of the LGBT+ community are more vulnerable to mental health issues, as he opens up about his own long-term battle with depression. Olly joins young people on their own personal journeys as they battle issues that parallel his own, from homophobic bullying to eating and anxiety disorders, and questions what can be done to address them

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Folkestone, Milton in Stoke-on-Trent, and Norbury in south London

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste return with more investigations into online dating scams, beginning with the exposure of an elaborate fraud

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A PCSO is attacked in Taunton with a pair of scissors while trying to apprehend a prolific offender, while police and paramedics explain the power of bodyworn video

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique hunting with two teams at the Royal Cheshire Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles heads to Knutsford's penny farthing museum

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al worries that Lily's latest move may have put his mother in danger, while Sid and Bear worry Daniel is having a mid-life crisis. Jimmi helps after a young woman dies at the university, but all is not what it seems.

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When the duo are hired to help close down an old Bingo Hall, Frank is surprised to find an old enemy at work - but what is he really up to? Guest starring Jim Moir, aka Vic Reeves, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an osteopath find a country retreat in Herefordshire, and visits an archaeological dig at Dorstone Hill which is revealing new facts about the Neolithic age

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea make clothes rails out of timber and copper piping, while archive clips include Danny Clarke reshaping a garden by carving out eyecatching flower beds

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: There are more than a million incidents a year of illegally dumped rubbish in the UK, so why are so few people prosecuted. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates a crime that affects everyone and meets some of those who are fighting back against the fly-tippers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil promises Kat there is nothing going on between him and Sharon. At the call centre, Mo spots Kheerat trying to poach a new customer, while Kim promises to get Isaac a date, determined for him to have a proper relationship. After Whitney passes on the offer - her own secret relationship in mind - Kim turns to another prospective love match

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Maya Jama, Roisin Conaty, Roman Kemp and Xand van Tulleken to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants showcase their celebrity impressions and improv skills as they take on the Snatch Game, with singer Jessie Ware joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Oscar-nominated British star Rosamund Pike, who discusses her new comedy thriller I Care a Lot. Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay chats about his new BBC quiz show Bank Balance, while Welsh rugby union great Gareth Thomas reflects on his honest and challenging new book, Stronger. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville talks about playing Roald Dahl in To Olivia, while comedian and presenter Judi Love also guests. Plus, Ella Henderson and Tom Grennan perform Let's Go Home Together

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate the spookier side of nature, including a tree that bleeds, a fungus that likes to eat ant brains and a flesh-chewing fly

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Crashed spaceships, and parents being beamed aboard a UFO dressed as space aliens - brother and sister Hattie and Bertie are planning a wedding that's out of this world

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A science den for a budding astrophysicist

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: With the mob now numbering 50, the burrow is full to bursting point and Rocket Dog's leadership comes under threat. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Following the death of Whiskers leader Flower, Maybelline and Rocket Dog battle it out for dominance of their respective groups and strive to regain control over them. As winter approaches it looks as if the manor might not be big enough for both of them. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Wilson makes a move to join the Aztecs group, but when his advances are thwarted by Zaphod, he seeks the attention of a female from the Whiskers clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as the second group of quarter-finalists give some scruffy bichons frisés their trademark white cloud look, and try to identify a cross-breed's two parents. In the final challenge, they must trim their dogs' coats to create a homage to a place close to their hearts, before showing off their creations on the Dogwalk. But who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor take through to next week?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the remote Stoer Peninsula in the North West Highlands, Helen and Graham are relieved to see tourists returning to the area, bringing a very welcome financial boost with them. However, they are soon forced to make a decision with devastating consequences. In Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan attempt to shear all their sheep, and in the Lake District, Matt and Murray embark on a dramatic gather

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1935)
programme.desc: A Canadian in London offers a safe hiding place to a British secret agent investigating a spy ring. However, when she is murdered, he realises he will be the prime suspect and goes on the run with the intention of exposing the real killers. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle and Peggy Ashcroft

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Amanda and Rustie meet a bride-to-be who invites the whole group to her Hindu wedding, so they head out to buy traditional clothing, with hilarious consequences. Miriam and Lionel discover Old Kochi has a surprising connection to their Jewish heritage, and a visit to the synagogue makes Miriam reflect on the impact her choice to marry outside of the Jewish faith had on her family. Later, Bill takes half the group along to one of the biggest festivals in Kerala, Puli Kali, which celebrates the tiger

programme.title: Tomorrowland: A World Beyond (2015)
programme.desc: A boy genius in the 1960s finds his way into a spectacular futuristic metropolis while attempting to promote his idea for a jetpack. Decades later, he has grown into a disillusioned adult, while a girl who has the talent and enthusiasm he has lost also finds her way into this strange new world, and together they attempt to uncover its secrets. Fantasy adventure, starring George Clooney, Britt Robertson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform a country wedding venue in Sussex. Presented by Alan Carr with Judge Michelle Ogundehin

programme.title: Perry and Croft: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The celebration of comedy writing duo David Croft and Jimmy Perry continues with an examination of how their sitcoms chronicled British people's attitudes to love and sex in the 20th century, looking at its depiction in Dad's Army, It Ain't Half Hot Mum, Hi-de-Hi! and You Rang, M'Lord?

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A major from Area HQ decides Corporal Jones is too old to be in the Home Guard, asking him to prove his worth by completing an assault course - leaving the rest of the platoon hatching a plan to help him. Comedy first broadcast in 1968, starring Clive Dunn, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Victoria Wood: A Bafta Tribute
programme.desc: Julie Walters, Lenny Henry, Richard E Grant and Jim Broadbent gather for a gala honouring the much-loved entertainer, with contributions from Peter Kay and French and Saunders. The celebration offers an opportunity to look back over her career with a combination of celebrity tributes and clips of her work, culminating with the presentation of the Bafta award

programme.title: Victoria Wood: At It Again
programme.desc: The much-loved comedian performs her award-winning stand-up show in front of an audience at London's Royal Albert Hall. Recorded during her At It Again tour in 2001, the gig features Victoria's ruminations on everything from Disneyland visitors to her hysterectomy

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren's mother wreaks havoc in the factory and incurs Stan's wrath by pursuing an ill-considered romance, while Anita organises a heated debate on the workers' current situation. Comedy, with Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters

programme.title: The Lighthouse (2016)
programme.desc: Two men are assigned to keep a lighthouse on an island in the Irish sea in 1801. A freak storm cuts the island off from the mainland for months on end, and the isolation drives both men to the brink of madness, with devastating consequences. Fact-based thriller, starring Mark Lewis Jones and Michael Jibson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Nikita defends her land when the Whiskers cross into Commando territory

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pregnancy causes problems for her rival Sophie, while Seacrest helps the Aztecs find a new home

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Zorro spots an opportunity to take over the Commandos when his roving crew runs into them, and Sophie faces a life in exile. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin travels to Usk in Monmouthshire, known as the Welsh 'town of flowers', where the community plans to fill the entire town square with a colourful new planting scheme

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Gloucestershire to pay visits to the gardens at Hidcote Manor and Kiftsgate Court. She cuts an underperforming shrub down to size, tackles an enormous rambling rose, and discovers the arts and crafts heritage of the Cotswolds

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway joins the 'surf dudes' when he visits St Bride's Bay, Pembrokeshire - but still needs a bit more practice. On his walk from Little Haven to Solva, he also learns about a UFO siting that stunned the world, brushes up on his life-saving skills, helps a local artist create a giant mural and is blown away by the stunning scenery

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penrith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts as the remaining three contestants take part in the final as they compete to be crowned the first-ever winner of the competition, with the cooks having to impress Mary Berry, Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin if they're to win the title. First, the competitors take on the Rustle Up challenge, creating dishes that showcase butternut squash as the main ingredient, before being asked to produce their ultimate picnic and presenting their creations to the judges at a beautifully decorated picnic tables. Who will be crowned the winner?

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by pioneering film director Alfred Hitchcock, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1938)
programme.desc: A traveller strikes up a conversation with a scatterbrained elderly lady when their train is delayed, but after the journey resumes, her new friend mysteriously vanishes. Almost everyone else on board pleads ignorance about the matter, prompting her and a fellow passenger to investigate the intriguing occurrence. Alfred Hitchcock's mystery, starring Margaret Lockwood, Michael Redgrave, May Whitty, Paul Lukas and Cecil Parker

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life of one of Hollywood's best-loved actresses, telling the story of her Oscar-winning career by using rarely seen archive footage of her appearances on the BBC

programme.title: Suspicion (1941)
programme.desc: A woman swept off her feet by the attentions of a charming rogue agrees to marry him, despite the warnings of friends. However, his increasingly odd behaviour arouses her suspicions, and when his business partner dies in mysterious circumstances, she becomes convinced he has murder in mind. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller, with Cary Grant, Joan Fontaine, Nigel Bruce and Cedric Hardwicke

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit the Yorkshire Dales to work at Swinton Estate, the ancestral home of Lord and Lady Masham. By taking a look the heart of the grand house and its countryside charm, as well as the wider estate, which has opened up to for guests to enjoy, Monica and Giles learn about the challenges of making history pay in the modern world

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Life in Cadgwith Cove, which is Cornwall's last traditional full-time fishing cove. For generations, sons have followed fathers off the beach and out to sea, and now the next generation is set to join the fleet. But while there might be new blood, the old working fabric of the cove itself is now under threat

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the conclusion of the Alpine World Championships from Cortina in Italy, featuring the men's slalom, in which Great Britain's Dave Ryding was looking to contend for a medal. Plus, a round-up of the rest of the action

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces the unique talents of the world's most intelligent animals, investigating how nature's masterminds measure up against human brain power. Looking at a variety of animals, such as ravens, crows, bees and starlings, Chris explores how different animals can use tools, solve complex puzzles, recognise themselves in mirrors and even reason like humans

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Rosie Jones and Guz Khan, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan spends a week exploring Colombia with a local radio host and journalist, discovering how much of the country's reputation for violence is justified. He investigates a new trend for narcotourism, where visitors seek out places associated with drug lord Pablo Escobar, and finds out how Colombians feel about foreigners' fascination with their nation's most infamous criminal. He also visits a Jesus statue shop, learns to make coffee the traditional way, and discovers a game involving explosions

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores how Scotland's landscape has shaped the nation's history. He begins on a trek from Aberdeen to Oban, meeting a hermit called Jake and exploring his very unique treehouse. He then visits the community of Findhorn, finding out how this remote area of barren sand dunes became transformed into a thriving village whose main aim is to be environmentally, economically and socially self-sustaining. Frankie also views one of the most stunning glens in the Highlands, giving his perspective on the Glencoe massacre of 1692, before going deep underground to visit the Cruachan Power Station

programme.title: A Monster Calls (2016)
programme.desc: Young Conor struggles to deal with his mother's terminal illness, the cold grandmother he has to stay with and the bullying he receives at school. He is woken from a recurring nightmare by the visitation of a tree monster at his bedroom window, telling him a series of stories. In return, Conor must tell the monster the truth behind his dreadful nightmare. Fantasy drama based on the award-winning book by Patrick Ness, starring Lewis MacDougall, Felicity Jones, Sigourney Weaver and Toby Kebbell, with the voice of Liam Neeson

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Angela reveals the dark secrets that have haunted her for years as Nathan, Peter, Claire and Noah help to uncover her past. Meanwhile, Suresh learns of his father's involvement in a long-forgotten government operation. Cristine Rose, Adrian Pasdar, Milo Ventimiglia and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar has an identity crisis as his ability takes its toll on him. Hiro and Ando take measures to bring down Building 26, while Matt's new-found fatherhood changes his priorities. Elsewhere, Nathan sets a plan in motion to right the wrongs of his government operation. Zachary Quinto, Masi Oka, James Kyson Lee and Greg Grunberg star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's life is hanging in the balance, while Hanssen questions whether he did the right thing. Ange is devastated when she learns that things have not gone to plan, and Dom will face life-changing consequences of his injuries. Sacha vows to save Jodie's baby, and begins to understand the ongoing impact of her relationship with her mother. Cameron announces his intention to make a career change

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: During her final days of pregnancy, Rocket Dog searches for a new burrow in which to give birth, while Zorro experiences life as a Commando

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups are put in jeopardy when babysitters Sophie and Axel become distracted by Wilson - before an even greater threat emerges

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea shows a crafty make to hang bathroom towels on, while Jay Blades builds an under-bed storage solution. Plus, Monty Don gives us the lowdown on the best hedge trimming tools, a dated armchair is given an outrageous upcycle, and Alan Titchmarsh shares his secrets on how to build a pond

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the remote Stoer Peninsula in the North West Highlands, Helen and Graham are relieved to see tourists returning to the area, bringing a very welcome financial boost with them. However, they are soon forced to make a decision with devastating consequences. In Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan attempt to shear all their sheep, and in the Lake District, Matt and Murray embark on a dramatic gather

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright is joined by social media star Saffron Barker to demonstrate exercises designed to get muscles moving

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bringing Up Baby (1938)
programme.desc: A zany heiress tries to acquire the help of a timid palaeontologist in looking after her pet leopard. As she starts to fall for the bemused academic, she decides to stop him marrying his severe assistant, and accidentally endangers his efforts to get funding for his beloved museum. Screwball comedy, starring Cary Grant, Katharine Hepburn, May Robson, Charlie Ruggles and Barry Fitzgerald

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley face-off at a London car boot sale. Kate gets hot under the collar when her whistling kettle is less than pitch perfect, and Caroline has an art attack as she decoupages some 1970s school chairs

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following Baghani, a tigress chosen to breed in the wild. She is airlifted from her home in an Indian park to a new reserve where she encounters Rajore, a male who has also recently been released. The pair are filmed over two years, as they fight leopards for territory and learn to hunt boar, while trying to avoid being killed by poachers

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to insects, whose key to success is an armoured covering that takes on almost any shape. Bombardier beetles fire boiling liquid at enemies through a rotating nozzle, while a honey bee army stings a raiding bear into submission. Grass-cutter ants march like a Roman army and cultivate a fungus to break down the grass they have harvested. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk is joined by chef Paul Ainsworth to celebrate food served to royal children, including cottage pie, said to be a favourite of Prince William

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson uncover a few gems including a silver ladle with a surprise in its bowl and a train set brought in by the son of William Moorcroft, the world-famous ceramicist. Plus, Paul finds out about the epic 18th-century voyage by the second Englishman to circumnavigate the globe

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge celebrates the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to dishes that are a little bit more indulgent. Mary begins by travelling to Scotland to enjoy the best of the country's staple foods, preparing recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, joins chef Tom Kitchin to share the joy of venison cooked on a barbecue and whips up her special whisky cream on the banks of a loch

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Sidney Poitier, Greek mythology, Soundgarden, and the Women's Institute., before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Dungeon Masters take on the Barons in the third of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of JG Farrell, Nipsey Hussle, Dag Hammarskjold, and Heath Ledger

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-final matches continue as two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: With more than 100 boats in the harbour, Newlyn is Cornwall's biggest fishing port and the outcome of the Brexit trade negotiations could transform the future for its fleet on inshore fishing boats. In the face of an uncertain future, the fleet must prepare for modernisation and whatever challenges or opportunities Brexit may bring

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Pointless co-host Alexander Armstrong takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up comedian Jon Richardson one of two guests joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Much of lockdown has been spent lamenting the loss of attending live performance across sport, music and theatre. Mary finds out why audiences matter and asks what role they play. She asks Ellie Simmonds and Clare Balding whether the loss of a crowd affects sports stars' performances, and attends her first Twitter listening party - a new way for musicians to reach their audience. She talks to Tim Burgess of the Charlatans, who came up with the idea, and the Rev Richard Coles, former keyboard player with 1980s band the Communards. Mary also presents a performance by Amy Macdonald from Glasgow's Barrowlands

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where items assessed include a letter from President Dwight D Eisenhower, a collection of Scottish silver and a Chinese table that is one of the most valuable ever seen on the programme. There is also a set of mystery objects to test even the most knowledgeable armchair expert

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Following his adventures at Everest Base Camp, the intrepid traveller leaves the mountains behind and moves on to Tibet. Here he discovers that, while there may be an increasing Chinese influence, religion is once again being tolerated. He visits the Potala Palace, the spectacular monasteries of Tashilunpo and takes a dip in a hot spring. From there he continues his journey with a family of nomads heading to a summer horse festival in Yushu

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: A diary dating from 1943 is discovered hidden in an old bunker and Montalbano is entrusted with the dark secrets revealed in the diary and starts to research the time around which it was written. During his investigation, a 90-year-old businessman is found murdered in his own home and the detective starts to think that the events cannot possibly be unrelated. Italian crime drama, starring Luca Zingaretti

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane -to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on August 23, with performances by the Human League, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, Bombalurina, Mariah Carey, Tina Turner, Lindy Layton featuring Janet Kay, Cliff Richard, Deee-Lite and Deacon Blue

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on August 30, with performances by artists including Aswad, Sonia, the KLF, Betty Boo, Jason Donovan, Bombalurina, Whitesnake, Steve Miller Band and New Kids on the Block

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable Top of the Pops performances from the 1980s, a time of outlandish hairstyles and dubious fashions. Featuring music by Adam Ant, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, Bucks Fizz, Yazz, Duran Duran, Wham! and Culture Club

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Following his adventures at Everest Base Camp, the intrepid traveller leaves the mountains behind and moves on to Tibet. Here he discovers that, while there may be an increasing Chinese influence, religion is once again being tolerated. He visits the Potala Palace, the spectacular monasteries of Tashilunpo and takes a dip in a hot spring. From there he continues his journey with a family of nomads heading to a summer horse festival in Yushu

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle

programme.title: The Many Faces of June Whitfield
programme.desc: The comedy actress tells the story of her career, focusing on her early days in the West End working with Noel Coward, her ill-fated Broadway debut and, with the help of rarely seen archive footage, her years of TV and radio performances. She also offers an insight into her on-screen relationship with Terry Scott and the secret behind her continuing success. With contributions by Sylvia Syms, Nicholas Parsons and June's daughter Suzy Aitchison

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: A peaceful ride through Vietnam comes to an abrupt end when the adventurer and his team find themselves on a small speedboat adrift in rough seas. Charley visits a famous site associated with the country's devastating war, the network of tunnels at Vinh Moc, as well as the largest waterfalls in South Asia. He then dirt bikes in the local countryside and tries wakeboarding across Singapore

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Margot
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn inspired generations of ballerinas, while her late-flowering partnership with Rudolf Nureyev created the most dazzling ballet partnership in history. However, her life was marked by tragedy and disappointment. Darcey finds that Margot couldn't find love and never had children, got drawn into a failed foreign revolution, danced on for too long, and died in poverty. The former ballerina goes behind the scenes at the Royal Opera House and the Royal Ballet, and travels from London to New York and Panama, discovering that Margot did eventually find a kind of happiness - though one that came at a high price

programme.title: Margot ()
programme.desc: Anne-Marie Duff stars as Margot Fonteyn in this drama exploring the ballerina's career and personal life. Along with the knowledge of her diplomat husband's infidelities, Margot senses mounting pressure from the Royal Ballet to consider retirement, but a professional partnership with Russian dancer Rudolf Nureyev turns her into a worldwide celebrity. With Michiel Huisman and Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Marguerite and Armand
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn and Rudolf Nureyev star in a performance of Frederick Ashton's ballet, which was inspired by Alexandre Dumas's tragic love story La Dame aux Camelias. The music is the B minor piano sonata by Liszt and the work is designed by Cecil Beaton

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. They include a rare collection of the first openly gay magazine - featuring the rising young singer David Bowie - to letters from worried parents of newly 'out' daughters and sons. Presented by Stephen K Amos and Susan Calman

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The second of two documentaries in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. Focusing on the past 30 years, it features the nun-impersonating freedom-fighting Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence, the writer behind a famous lesbian kiss and a Muslim man who set up an LGBTQ support group for South East Asians. Presented by Susan Calman and Stephen K Amos

programme.title: The Many Faces of June Whitfield
programme.desc: The comedy actress tells the story of her career, focusing on her early days in the West End working with Noel Coward, her ill-fated Broadway debut and, with the help of rarely seen archive footage, her years of TV and radio performances. She also offers an insight into her on-screen relationship with Terry Scott and the secret behind her continuing success. programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: A peaceful ride through Vietnam comes to an abrupt end when the adventurer and his team find themselves on a small speedboat adrift in rough seas. Charley visits a famous site associated with the country's devastating war, the network of tunnels at Vinh Moc, as well as the largest waterfalls in South Asia. He then dirt bikes in the local countryside and tries wakeboarding across Singapore

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A profile of some of the steeplejack's favourite Victorian engineers, reflecting his interest in their belief in the importance of hard work and enterprise. The documentary also highlights how Fred's idols eventually received greater recognition for their achievements

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a little wooden shanty upon a bed of soft snow in the middle of a cold, wintry forest

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate a landscape that may be a lost painting by Constable. The painting is currently owned by a Gloucestershire businessman and seems to have all the hallmarks of Constable's early sketches, as well as clear signs that it depicts the same location as The Hay Wain - but was dismissed as another artist's work 30 years previously. The case proves particularly personal for Philip, who is one of the artwork's previous owners

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: After finding a broken surfboard on his local beach, Jhonny Guerrero, a teenager from one of Peru's toughest barrios, sets his heart on becoming a professional surfer. This documentary follows Jhonny as surf champion Sofia Mulanovich takes him under her wing and gives him a chance to succeed in the sport

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Geologist Simon Haslett and tsunami expert Ted Bryant reveal the devastation caused to Britain when a 12m-high wall of water hit an area near Bristol in 1607 and claimed 2,000 lives. It crashed ashore with such ferocity that the geography of the coastline was altered for ever, yet despite being chronicled by eyewitnesses, the country's worst natural disaster has been forgotten with time

programme.title: Titian - Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The story behind the artist's commission for Philip II of Spain, known as the poesie paintings, which had not been seen together for over 450 years and had been the subject of an exhibition at London's National Gallery before it was closed due to the Covid-19 outbreak. Cameras were recently granted access to bring this groundbreaking exhibition to the screens across Britain and beyond

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: David Malone investigates the importance of ocean waves. He discovers they are not made of water, can travel sideways and their sound is created by bubbles, and examines the connection between the life cycles of waves and human beings

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a little wooden shanty upon a bed of soft snow in the middle of a cold, wintry forest

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There is a turtle expert visiting the Safari World aquarium. Mr Hammond is on a scuba diving holiday in Borneo, so it’s up to Andy to ensure everything is ready for their arrival. Andy does a great job making the aquarium looking its best, but when Mr Hammond asks Andy if he has cleaned the green sea turtles, Andy is a little confused as he thought they cleaned themselves. Mr Hammond informs Jen and Andy that green sea turtles get clean by letting little fish nibble away the dirt and parasites on their skin and shell. However, he neglects to tell them what kind of fish, so Andy decides to find out for himself. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she discovers that astronauts live in space on a spacecraft called the International Space Station and that there have been different astronauts living there for over 20 years. Maddie shows us how astronauts live and work on the International Space Station and do lots of different jobs and research in order to learn more about space and our planet Earth. We explore a laboratory where they do experiments, the food they eat, how they wash and even how they go to the toilet. We find out that the space station is made from thousands of parts that were put together by astronauts in space, and we learn that it’s also a home because it’s a place where astronauts eat, sleep and have fun.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. Mr Hammond is on a wildlife trip in New Zealand, and Andy is eager to hear what animals he has spotted. He informs Jen and Andy that he has seen killer whales and is fishing for trout. Andy reassures a distressed Mr Hammond that he and Jen will investigate why the seal pup is making its way into the forest. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Activity ideas for you and your toddler all about moving and dancing! See how encouraging your toddler to move is great for their development. Join in and get moving! Hosted by Giovanna from her own home.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee gives the squirrels a big drink of water after they have been running around

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe meets her cousin Bethan and her guide Sparky for a picnic in the park

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Stompy Chomper runs around hungrily eating rocks. Can B.O.T. succeed in this mission?

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Jago the Robin has been practicing his song for weeks. Finally, it’s ready and he’s very excited to perform it to his friends. But no one is able to listen to him! Betty the Mouse is too busy looking after her little ones, pigs Thelma and Louise are asleep and water voles Walter and Winnie are too noisy, so no one can hear Jago sing above the racket they’re making! Will he ever get to sing his song to an audience?

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is at home, where today he has been writing in his diary.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on an early morning walk to spot some cloud shapes. On the way, they see the city waking up, find a funny mirror, spot shapes in the clock tower and even catch a big fish!

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo is fascinated by the wrinkly hands of his elderly neighbour.

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a bucket and mop coming out of a ball and a drawing man falling in love with a ballerina, and watch as Book Boy makes a slide by doing magic.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Phinehas and Charlie as they both wait for their newborn baby brother or sister to arrive into the world. Charlie’s mum packs a bag ready to go to hospital with dad and waves goodbye to Charlie and Grandma, whilst Phinehas’ family gets ready to pop a gender reveal balloon to find out if the next arrival will be a boy or a girl. Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. Charlie meets a health visitor, who has come to visit to check on the baby and make sure it is staying healthy and strong.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Firefighter Karen shows us her fabulous helmet. We see how important it is for Karen to wear it while she does her heroic job, as well as some of the amazing things it does.

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Olobobs eagerly await the arrival of some snow, but the lazy snowclouds are sleeping. They make Ticky Tocky, who wakes the clouds so they can finally play

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Naomi makes a very special Senegalese seafood dish called Mafe Fish. She serves it with white fluffy rice and a traditional, colourful Senegalese drink

programme.title: CBeebies Presents
programme.desc: Adaptation of Lewis Carroll's classic tale in which a young girl learns to use her imagination as she journeys through a fairy-tale land. Staged at the Wales Millennium Centre, Cardiff, with a cast featuring numerous CBeebies stars, including Justin Fletcher, Ben Faulks, Katrina Bryan, Phil Gallagher and Pui Fan Lee

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'octopus', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a giant octopus, a tiny octopus, a swimming octopus and a gardening octopus! Dee meets a spinning octopus and a pirate octopus - arrrrgh! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: OO and friends discover a talking book, but some words are missing

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: When Nine needs a helping hand, he turns into a talented trio of circus blocks, the Three Threes. Plus, a look at how to share nine into equal parts

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog finds the original book that starts all of his adventures. He shows Pug how he started the bookshop and how he read all the books, so he could recommend which books each customer should read. It was a great success but Pug was not in this story.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy monster cannot find his caterpillar friends, and the garden bush where he left his friends is now covered with pretty butterflies

programme.title: Mister Maker at Home
programme.desc: Mister Maker is having trouble using his trusty Makerphone and there’s only one friend who will be best placed to help him. He video-calls his friend Jen from CBeebies’s Andy’s Aquatic Adventures, which leads to arty inspiration! Mister Maker makes a pretend computer out of lots of card, stickers, fun foam and buttons. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister M makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art - he turns an old sock into something special! Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes to the theatre with his friends to watch a sensory performance. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble puts on a magic show for Aunt Polly and Lord Tumble

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies eat lots of Tubby Toast and make a huge pile of crumbs

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny tries to help a butterfly that has flown inside the nursery and landed on Sula's painting

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo keeps getting calls on her mobile phone, and after a while Tee gets fed up because he cannot attract her attention

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan visits a farm as workers prepare their pigs for a visit from the vet. Rory Crawford helps a vet with an examination on some amazing alpacas

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Three young brothers have fun blowing bubbles in the garden

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are really little!

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook, Line and Captain Captain collect precious things that remind them of good times on the Scarlet Squid. Molly, Lola, Connie and Noah play Stepping Stones and Tumbly Barrel before taking on the Shipwreck Rummage to try and win back Gem's jewels

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: On a hot day, lots of people decide to visit a tropical lake and there's a traffic jam. It looks like Duggee and the Squirrels might be stuck for some time, but thankfully Duggee has his traffic badge and knows what to do

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Jemima's new egg goes missing, and only Peter Rabbit is able to put together the clues to find the culprit - but must find the egg before it hatches

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Alexandra shows us her barrister’s wig. She explains why she loves her wig so much and what it means to be able to wear it.

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Zola-Rose discovers lots of interesting facts about super-slow snails! Rory and his son Sandy search for snails in the garden and put on a very exciting snail race, while Ferne meets some very rare snails and holds a Giant African Land Snail at the zoo. Plus, we find out why snails are so important to the environment. They play an important part in a giant food web.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Safari World is opening the Arctic Dome to visitors and Mr Hammond has chosen animal costumes for Andy and Jen to wear while they show people around. Jen informs Mr Hammond and Andy that there is trouble in the Arctic Dome with the walrus’s not having enough space due to a recent baby boom. Mr Hammond is worried that the walrus pups will get squished and can’t work out why there is not enough space. Andy suggests Mr Hammond goes and entertains the visitors while he and Jen work out what’s wrong. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile, jetting off to the Arctic. Jen picks up some activity not far from the safari-mobile, so she suggests that’s a good place to start. Andy discovers that Jen

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch transform's Sydney's iconic Opera House into an opera boat for a race around the Australian city's harbour

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack tries to help Daisy by bringing Twinkle the pony to the vet's surgery. Twinkle escapes and ends up in the Big Hub - which doesn't please Moira at all

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle tries to be a purr-fect house guest for Mrs Hobbs but he misses the Brooklyn-Bells

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran suggests they post JoJo's picture to Great Gran Gran in Saint Lucia. JoJo learns why a letter takes such a long time to travel halfway across the world.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The duo discover some unusual inhabitants in the plant section while at the big shop

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: A nursery rhyme book inspires Mr Onion to play at being Humpty Dumpty.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major Clanger makes a new garden trolley for Mother

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy's bed follows the friends into the garden, but runs away from her when she wants to sleep

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Chris Hoy reads Giraffe on a Bicycle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Yotam Ottolenghi, and special guests Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt. Plus, Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton and Arsenal v Manchester City

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Welsh Open
programme.desc: Ian Hunt presents coverage of the opening semi-final on the sixth day at Celtic Manor in Newport, played over the best of 11 frames. Kyren Wilson edged out Ronnie O'Sullivan 6-5 at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Dominic Dale, Ken Doherty and Darren Morgan

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Impressionist Jon Culshaw, Richie Anderson from BBC Radio 2, broadcaster Rachel Johnson and actor Denis Lawson answer questions on Doctor Who, The Saturdays, The Laconia incident and Miles Davis

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, England goalkeepers David James and Siobhan Chamberlain take on rugby legend James Haskell and fitness coach Chloe Madeley

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees footballing legend John Barnes and football pundit Chris Kamara teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Peter Purves, Lesley Judd, Jane Tucker, Freddy Marks, Dave Benson Phillips, Mike Read, Kirsten O'Brien and Terrence Hardiman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha and Ethan have kept their distance since Fenisha had the baby, but when their son is rushed into hospital, Fenisha realises that Ethan will be there to support her. Meanwhile, Connie is shocked by Grace's behaviour, and Rosa encourages Ollie to make a move. Starring George Rainsford, Olivia D'Lima and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton is attacked while on remand in prison awaiting trial and tells Fleming she had a relationship with a high-ranking officer - who may have reason to want her framed. After AC-12 gains access to the files on the protected witness, the evidence suggests a possible police conspiracy and the cohesion of the team is further threatened by the arrival of a former adversary. Crime drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. Everton were looking for their first win away to their neighbours since 1999 and will have drawn confidence from some recent home defeats for the Reds. Southampton were looking to get back on track after a humiliating 9-0 defeat at the hands of Manchester United 17 days ago, while the Blues were hoping to move up the table under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Plus, two crucial matches in the battle to avoid relegation - Burnley v West Bromwich Albion and Fulham v Sheffield United

programme.title: Stronger (2017)
programme.desc: A man's life changes radically after he is seriously injured in the 2013 Boston Marathon Bombings. Jeff Bauman loses both his legs in the tragedy, but captures the hearts of his city and the world through his recovery, as he adjusts to his new way of life. Drama based on a true story, starring Jake Gyllenhaal

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. Everton were looking for their first win away to their neighbours since 1999 and will have drawn confidence from some recent home defeats for the Reds. Southampton were looking to get back on track after a humiliating 9-0 defeat at the hands of Manchester United 17 days ago, while the Blues were hoping to move up the table under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Plus, two crucial matches in the battle to avoid relegation - Burnley v West Bromwich Albion and Fulham v Sheffield United

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley introduces services for the season of religious observance of Lent, beginning with the Rev Mark Bradford leading one at St Cuthbert's Church in Fulwood, Lancashire. Hymns and songs include In Christ Alone, Standing in the Need of Prayer and When I Survey the Wondrous Cross, sung by Faye Simpson, Katie Ritson and the Adventist Vocal Ensemble

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Rosins sample life in Brisbane for a week, but while Julia's dream is to move to Australia in the hope of a better lifestyle for their children, husband Hussein is not so sure

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young help two teams challenged to scour an antiques fair to pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Charlie Ross hosts, and also pays a visit to the Norfolk Tank Museum

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Welsh Open
programme.desc: Ian Hunt presents coverage of the opening session of the final on the seventh day at Celtic Manor in Newport, as the first eight frames take place. Shaun Murphy dominated this session last year in Cardiff, taking a 7-1 lead. With commentary and analysis from Dominic Dale, Ken Doherty, Darren Morgan and Rob Walker

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Following Jez and Ieuan, two young men from Llanelli who had a dream to change their lives and become pizza-makers extraordinaire. The pair decided to buy an old Italian van, put an oven in the back and set off on an adventure to enter the World Pizza Championships in Parma. In the first episode, the pair get some last minute tips from their favourite Cardiff pizzeria, host their own special send-off before packing up their van, Smokey Pete, and hitting the road

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap explores Northamptonshire's Nene Valley, finding out how a farmer who was fighting flooding is now embracing wetland wildlife to make his land pay. Adam Henson hopes for good news from his ewes as he discovers how many are in lamb, while Charlotte Smith investigates whether Brexit is delivering all it promised for the UK's fishing industry and ecologist Josh Styles reveals the secret world inside a prehistoric plant

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Windermere Jetty Museum in the Lake District, where items assessed include a collection of silver trophies awarded to a pioneering female sailor. There is also a James Bond poster scraped off a wall, a guitar rescued from a skip, a wooden dingy from the film Swallows & Amazons, and a pair of necklaces with surprising values

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: While investigating when a car belonging to a former IRA member is pulled from Strangford Lough, DCI Tom Brannick recognises the calling card of a serial killer from 20 years ago known as Goliath - one of whose victims was the detective's wife. Despite opposition from old friend DCS Jackie Twomey, Brannick and partner DS Niamh McGovern break open the cold case in the hope that it will help them. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt, with Charlene McKenna and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Arsenal v Manchester City at Etihad Stadium, Aston Villa v Leicester City at Villa Park, West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium and Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Olly Alexander: Growing Up Gay
programme.desc: The Years & Years lead singer explores why members of the LGBT+ community are more vulnerable to mental health issues, as he opens up about his own long-term battle with depression. Olly joins young people on their own personal journeys as they battle issues that parallel his own, from homophobic bullying to eating and anxiety disorders, and questions what can be done to address them

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Folkestone, Milton in Stoke-on-Trent, and Norbury in south London

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste return with more investigations into online dating scams, beginning with the exposure of an elaborate fraud

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A PCSO is attacked in Taunton with a pair of scissors while trying to apprehend a prolific offender, while police and paramedics explain the power of bodyworn video

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al worries that Lily's latest move may have put his mother in danger, while Sid and Bear worry Daniel is having a mid-life crisis. Jimmi helps after a young woman dies at the university, but all is not what it seems.

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When the duo are hired to help close down an old Bingo Hall, Frank is surprised to find an old enemy at work - but what is he really up to? Guest starring Jim Moir, aka Vic Reeves, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an osteopath find a country retreat in Herefordshire, and visits an archaeological dig at Dorstone Hill which is revealing new facts about the Neolithic age

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea make clothes rails out of timber and copper piping, while archive clips include Danny Clarke reshaping a garden by carving out eyecatching flower beds

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The team reports on the online marketing schemes making extravagant nutritional claims, and heads to Snowdonia to find out about how the issue of light pollution is being tackled, as well as investigating gadgets that claim to help reduce it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil promises Kat there is nothing going on between him and Sharon. At the call centre, Mo spots Kheerat trying to poach a new customer, while Kim promises to get Isaac a date, determined for him to have a proper relationship. After Whitney passes on the offer - her own secret relationship in mind - Kim turns to another prospective love match

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Maya Jama, Roisin Conaty, Roman Kemp and Xand van Tulleken to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: There are more than a million incidents a year of illegally dumped rubbish in the UK, so why are so few people prosecuted. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates a crime that affects everyone and meets some of those who are fighting back against the fly-tippers

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants showcase their celebrity impressions and improv skills as they take on the Snatch Game, with singer Jessie Ware joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.desc: Action from the meeting in Torun, Poland, including the women's 60m, 3,000m and 800m, and men's 60m hurdles, pole vault and 1500m. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the women's singles final at Melbourne Park in Australia, which was won by Sofia Kenin last year

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Retired couple David and Joyce's back garden in their home outside Gloucester looks more like an allotment than a garden. David is a dedicated vegetable grower and for Joyce, that means there's nowhere shady or pretty to sit and enjoy the garden. Meanwhile, David's increasing back and knee problems mean he is struggling to keep up its maintenance. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set out to make the garden both prettier and more user friendly, all within a £3,000 budget

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay

programme.title: Sunday Worship
