programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes Yorkshireman Ged, who has brought in a classic piece of industrial furniture saved from the scrap heap. Henrie wants to sell a silver and mahogany Chinese inkwell from the early 19th century. And Molly from Manchester is selling a a watchman's timepiece

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits L'Abri fellowship in Hampshire to explore Christian faith and hospitality. Plus, music from Laura Wright, who performs with the Choir of Royal Holloway

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the men's singles final at Melbourne Park in Australia, which was won by Novak Djokovic for a record-extending eighth time last year

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers head to in Trowbridge, Wiltshire, to help Jacqui and Yung, who have never had time to fix up the tired garden of their new home

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap explores Northamptonshire's Nene Valley, finding out how a farmer who was fighting flooding is now embracing wetland wildlife to make his land pay. Adam Henson hopes for good news from his ewes as he discovers how many are in lamb, while Charlotte Smith investigates whether Brexit is delivering all it promised for the UK's fishing industry and ecologist Josh Styles reveals the secret world inside a prehistoric plant

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Windermere Jetty Museum in the Lake District, where items assessed include a collection of silver trophies awarded to a pioneering female sailor. There is also a James Bond poster scraped off a wall, a guitar rescued from a skip, a wooden dingy from the film Swallows & Amazons, and a pair of necklaces with surprising values

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: While investigating when a car belonging to a former IRA member is pulled from Strangford Lough, DCI Tom Brannick recognises the calling card of a serial killer from 20 years ago known as Goliath - one of whose victims was the detective's wife. Despite opposition from old friend DCS Jackie Twomey, Brannick and partner DS Niamh McGovern break open the cold case in the hope that it will help them. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt, with Charlene McKenna and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents highlights of today's Premiership fixtures - Rangers v Dundee United and Ross County v Celtic at Global Energy Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day 2

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: There are more than a million incidents a year of illegally dumped rubbish in the UK, so why are so few people prosecuted. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates a crime that affects everyone and meets some of those who are fighting back against the fly-tippers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil promises Kat there is nothing going on between him and Sharon. At the call centre, Mo spots Kheerat trying to poach a new customer, while Kim promises to get Isaac a date, determined for him to have a proper relationship. After Whitney passes on the offer - her own secret relationship in mind - Kim turns to another prospective love match

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Maya Jama, Roisin Conaty, Roman Kemp and Xand van Tulleken to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants showcase their celebrity impressions and improv skills as they take on the Snatch Game, with singer Jessie Ware joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Oscar-nominated British star Rosamund Pike, who discusses her new comedy thriller I Care a Lot. Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay chats about his new BBC quiz show Bank Balance, while Welsh rugby union great Gareth Thomas reflects on his honest and challenging new book, Stronger. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville talks about playing Roald Dahl in To Olivia, while comedian and presenter Judi Love also guests. Plus, Ella Henderson and Tom Grennan perform Let's Go Home Together

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Yotam Ottolenghi, and special guests Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt. Plus, Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton and Arsenal v Manchester City

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the meeting in Torun, Poland, including the women's 60m, 3,000m and 800m, and men's 60m hurdles, pole vault and 1500m. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the women's singles final at Melbourne Park in Australia, which was won by Sofia Kenin last year

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Retired couple David and Joyce's back garden in their home outside Gloucester looks more like an allotment than a garden. David is a dedicated vegetable grower and for Joyce, that means there's nowhere shady or pretty to sit and enjoy the garden. Meanwhile, David's increasing back and knee problems mean he is struggling to keep up its maintenance. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set out to make the garden both prettier and more user friendly, all within a £3,000 budget

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Impressionist Jon Culshaw, Richie Anderson from BBC Radio 2, broadcaster Rachel Johnson and actor Denis Lawson answer questions on Doctor Who, The Saturdays, The Laconia incident and Miles Davis

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, England goalkeepers David James and Siobhan Chamberlain take on rugby legend James Haskell and fitness coach Chloe Madeley

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees footballing legend John Barnes and football pundit Chris Kamara teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Peter Purves, Lesley Judd, Jane Tucker, Freddy Marks, Dave Benson Phillips, Mike Read, Kirsten O'Brien and Terrence Hardiman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha and Ethan have kept their distance since Fenisha had the baby, but when their son is rushed into hospital, Fenisha realises that Ethan will be there to support her. Meanwhile, Connie is shocked by Grace's behaviour, and Rosa encourages Ollie to make a move. Starring George Rainsford, Olivia D'Lima and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton is attacked while on remand in prison awaiting trial and tells Fleming she had a relationship with a high-ranking officer - who may have reason to want her framed. After AC-12 gains access to the files on the protected witness, the evidence suggests a possible police conspiracy and the cohesion of the team is further threatened by the arrival of a former adversary. Crime drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. Everton were looking for their first win away to their neighbours since 1999 and will have drawn confidence from some recent home defeats for the Reds. Southampton were looking to get back on track after a humiliating 9-0 defeat at the hands of Manchester United 17 days ago, while the Blues were hoping to move up the table under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Plus, two crucial matches in the battle to avoid relegation - Burnley v West Bromwich Albion and Fulham v Sheffield United

programme.title: Stronger (2017)
programme.desc: A man's life changes radically after he is seriously injured in the 2013 Boston Marathon Bombings. Jeff Bauman loses both his legs in the tragedy, but captures the hearts of his city and the world through his recovery, as he adjusts to his new way of life. Drama based on a true story, starring Jake Gyllenhaal

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Right now the world is seeing two sides of Vladimir Putin's Russia. The one he wants you to see is the scientifically advanced nation offering the world an effective Covid vaccine known as Sputnik V. The one he'd rather you ignore is the repressive authoritarian state that ruthlessly eliminates those who threaten the status quo. Stephen Sackur speaks to Kiril Dmitriev a Putin ally, the boss of one of Russia's sovereign wealth funds and a key backer of the Russian vaccine. Is Russia ripe for change?

programme.desc: Memories of moving up a class in primary school, and adjusting to a new position as older children in the social structure

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads return from a music festival having sampled a variety of dishes at Street Food stalls. Inspired by what they tasted they cook up their own take on fast and tasty foods

programme.title: Donny B: The Original King of Daytime
programme.desc: The story of broadcaster and former BBC Pebble Mill at One presenter Donny B MacLeod, looking back at the archive of his work and what led him to his career choice. Featuring contributions by his family and friends

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: A look at the lives of some of Glasgow's young generation of Gaels in Scotland's biggest city

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave demands to go on a foreign exchange. He soon finds himself headed for a week-long stay in a flat a few doors down.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: George finds a computer called Val but Val has a mind of her own. Soon she's set about uploading embarrassing videos to social media.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Gordon responds to an emergency along the Supreme Barrier Reef. The Tracy brothers once again set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood in an updated version of the show

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Fast flowing waters await Danny in today's dare as he takes on a white-water rafting course including the UK's steepest man-made drop.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean arranges a last-minute birthday treat for Teddy, including a morning in a toy shop and burgers for lunch. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean's landlady orders him to get rid of a mole making a mess of her exquisitely manicured croquet lawn

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: There's a new sheriff in town when Mike attempts to stop the raccoons from using Tom's train set to ship their loot back to their den.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series based on the popular film series. Mavis is 115 years old which is the age a Dracula finally earns their cape. But it won't be easy.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids make it to Gongmen City in search of the contact that they heard has some information about the airship crash - meanwhile, Xiao, mourning the presumed death of her new panda friends, takes off into the woods and meets a new one.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald and chefs Brian Turner and Paul Ainsworth. James prepares carrot soup and banoffee pithivier, and provides a masterclass in filleting fish

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite classic French dishes, from steak and red wine jus, to a heritage salad with tomatoes and mozzarella straight from his gardens. He also receives an unexpected visit from Jean-Christophe Novelli, who prepares him a delicious Mouclade Des Boucholeurs

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The east coast stretch of his journey takes James to The Hamptons. Known as being the playground of wealthy New Yorkers, the area has a rich farming history. James cooks for some local farmers before going out on a lobster boat and cooking along the waterside

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of four races from Ascot, including the 3.35 Grade One Betfair Chase. Plus, two races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Grand National Trial, and the Kingwell Hurdle from Wincanton. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: A chance to relive some of the most dramatic end games in the arcade-themed quiz show's history, including agonising trades and jackpot counters teetering on the edge. Presented by Ben Shephard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Claire, Steve and Jane pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Tom Allen, Fleur East and Simon Gregson all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show returns for a new series. Gary Barlow is live in the studio for an incredible End of The Show Show, Stephen Mulhern returns for an epic Ant v Dec, Fleur East is ready to give one viewer the surprise of their lives, and Harry and Jamie Redknapp are the victims of a brand new I'm A Celebrity Get Out Of Me Ear

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the first of this year's Battle Rounds in the singing contest. Coaches will.i.am, Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Anne-Marie all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen from the blind auditions go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Amongst the guests featured are Oprah Winfrey, Boris Johnson, Sandra Bullock, Tom Hanks, One Direction, Michael McIntyre, Dame Joan Collins, Big Narstie, Danny Dyer, James Corden, Carrie Fisher, Russell Howard and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies (1997)
programme.desc: James Bond joins forces with a Chinese agent to foil a media mogul's scheme to start a war between their countries - a case complicated by the fact that an old flame is now married to his new enemy. Using an undetectable stealth ship, the press baron plans to launch an undetectable sneak attack on both nations' navies, knowing they will blame each other - giving him exclusive global media coverage of the resulting conflict. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Jonathan Pryce, Teri Hatcher and Michelle Yeoh

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud

programme.desc: The clumsy hero is kept awake by the deafening sound of roadworks outside his bedroom window, but a plot to restore peace by feeding the workmen goes spectacularly wrong

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The clumsy hero sets out to replace his worn-out sofa, but finds he does not have enough cash for a new one

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike hopes to win over Iris by showering her with roses, and decides the best way to do that is by borrowing Stephan's leaf blower

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series based on the popular film series. Mavis is 115 years old which is the age a Dracula finally earns their cape. But it won't be easy.

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Batgirl's problems multiply, while Catwoman makes some new friends of her own.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter takes on a dramatic river walk along the Dart Valley in Devon, from the market town of Totnes to the naval town of Dartmouth. Along the route, Julia learns about re-wilding at the Sharpham Estate, helps harvest Pinot Noir grapes, and takes a look inside Greenway - the secluded home of Agatha Christie

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes Harry Redknapp, Security Correspondent Frank Gardner and actress Sarah Hadland to The Farm, and celebrates the best of British rum and stunning hedge rows

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite classic French dishes, from steak and red wine jus, to a heritage salad with tomatoes and mozzarella straight from his gardens. He also receives an unexpected visit from Jean-Christophe Novelli, who prepares him a delicious Mouclade Des Boucholeurs

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: The team are in Hackney, where they surprise Jeroume and Onessa Cariazo, the parents of wheelchair-bound nine-year-old Elijah, who suffers from myotubular myopathy. Alan Titchmarsh, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney give Elijah the sensory stimulation he craves, transforming the family's uneven overgrown urban courtyard into an accessible paradise complete with tropical water feature, a sensory beach and Asian-style dining zone

programme.title: Jurassic Park (1993)
programme.desc: Scientists are given a sneak preview of a safari park inhabited by genetically engineered dinosaurs created by an eccentric tycoon. However, it all goes wrong when a greedy employee sabotages the security systems, allowing the prehistoric beasts to roam free and hunt the visitors. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, starring Sam Neill, Laura Dern, Jeff Goldblum, Richard Attenborough and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Giles Coren, Rachel Johnson, Chris Packham and Josie Long pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice: The Greatest Show on Ice
programme.desc: A selection of memorable moments from the skating challenge with Torvill and Dean

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat, with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Alice needs to make enough money to save the house and find a way to mend bridges with her family. However, when her parents' marriage falls to pieces, Roger decides to leave Sarah and becomes Ms Dillon's latest uninvited lodger. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo embark on the final leg of their European road trip as they explore more of Italy, engaging in gladiatorial combat in Rome, before continuing south and making a stop to soak up the views from a sky-high zipwire. As they reach their final stop on the Amalfi Coast, they get the chance to swim in the sea, but they'll have to dive in from a cliff

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Former football manager Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort, offering an insight into this unique part of the British south coast peninsula. In the first episode, Harry is joined by Neil 'Razor' Ruddock and Joe Pasquale. Neil is considering a move to the area, so Harry takes him and Joe to see a very desirable £5million penthouse with some of the best views Sandbanks has to offer

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet. The final episode takes Joanna from North Wales to St Michael's Mount and the West Country, ending in her home city of London

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Snetterton, featuring round 16 of the GT4 Supercup and round 14 of the Porsche Carrera Cup

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts as five family members answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Paul, Alex, Catherine and Harriett pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Juliette is back and wants to see Carl. Laurel worries about Gabby's future. Nate tries to ready himself

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah is pleased when Lucas calls at Underworld to do business, but Carla masks her discomfort. Steve is put out to discover that Eileen's arranged for Tim to drive the hearse at Ted's funeral. Simon shows off to Jacob, telling him about his criminal past as a gang member

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh makes over a garden in Newark, Nottinghamshire, for a double-amputee veteran Nathan Cumberland and his young family. Despite his injuries, through his work with the Defence Medical Welfare Service charity, Nathan has devoted his life outside the armed forces to supporting older and elderly veterans who are often less likely to seek help for themselves than their younger counterparts. With tips on how to create a beautiful woodland themed outdoor space, Alan, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney take on Nathan's inaccessible plot and transform it into a show-stopping family garden

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Unaware that he overheard her conversation with Lucas, Carla is bewildered to see Peter so down. Alya opens the pile of final demands with mounting concern. George gives Steve and Tim a dry run at driving the hearse to see who's best

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: A dismembered body is found in a scrapyard and the team believes the remains had been stored in a domestic freezer for 30 years. Meanwhile, after making the decision to retire from the police force, Cassie faces a dilemma when she learns she isn't entitled to her full pension payment unless she completes her 30 years of service. Return of the crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Former boxer Chris Eubank talks about his private life and career, touching on his teenage years as a shoplifter, his divorce, bankruptcy and his victory in 1990 over fierce rival Nigel Benn. Chris also recalls the 1991 fight in which his opponent Michael Watson received a near-fatal brain injury, and discusses the success of his son, Chris Eubank Jr

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets an inspiring foster dad determined to transform his unhealthy lifestyle for the sake of his children. Plus, a teenager hoping to turn her difficult childhood experiences into something positive by joining the police force. And a brave grandmother whose confidence was destroyed by an accident is desperate to reclaim her independence

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh makes over a garden in Newark, Nottinghamshire, for a double-amputee veteran Nathan Cumberland and his young family. Despite his injuries, through his work with the Defence Medical Welfare Service charity, Nathan has devoted his life outside the armed forces to supporting older and elderly veterans who are often less likely to seek help for themselves than their younger counterparts. With tips on how to create a beautiful woodland themed outdoor space, Alan, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney take on Nathan's inaccessible plot and transform it into a show-stopping family garden

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. This time, the hotel hosts a series of BAFTA-related events, which sees a multitude of celebrities and film stars from across the globe coming in and out of the hotel. Head butler Sean shows newest butler Michael the ropes about discretion and how to look after the hotel's most important guests

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys)

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police dig up Kim's garden looking for the money. Mandy tells Butch he has to smarten himself up for the wedding. Terry and Alan have a showdown in the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy confronts Mandy about the wedding. Kim visits Steve at the remand centre. Butch enlists Biff's help to change his image. Chelsea continues bullying Robert at school

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les panics when he wins big on the races but cannot find the ticket. Deirdre and Jon look for a house to buy. Nick and Leanne meet in secret

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les spreads the news that Ken is a pervert. Liz is not happy about her 40th birthday approaching. Kevin is not keen when Natalie invites him to a dinner party with her friends

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: After finding out at the age of 19 that the man who raised him was not his real dad, former British soldier David Smith yearns to find the biological father he has never met. After years of dreaming, he hopes to finally know what it feels like to be called 'son'. Meanwhile, Mandy Hearn, who was adopted as a baby in 1968, hopes to trace her birth mother. However, at just 13 years old when she gave birth, she is the youngest biological mother the programme has ever encountered. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Fact-based drama starring David Tennant as notorious serial killer Dennis Nilsen. On 9 February 1983, Detective Chief Inspector Peter Jay is called to the leafy north London address of 23 Cranley Gardens, where he discovers the drains clogged with rotting human flesh and bones. But the extent of this grisly discovery only becomes apparent when the culprit returns from work. Dennis Nilsen, a meek man, freely admits to the police that it's not just one or two bodies, but as many as 15 or 16. As the police try to secure a conviction and identify the victims from scant DNA evidence, biographer Brian Masters attempts to understand why. A game of chess with 'Des' begins, and while Brian may be exhilarated by his newest subject, he's unaware what this new obsession could do to his life. Also starring Daniel Mays, Jason Watkins and Barry Ward

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Crime drama following the personal and professional lives of two detectives working for a Manchester police unit that specialises in murder cases. DC Janet Scott (Lesley Sharp) and DC Rachel Bailey (Suranne Jones) investigate the case of a pregnant woman who was killed in her bedroom. Meanwhile, Rachel's love life takes a turn for the worse and Janet gets a call that reawakens a past tragedy. Also starring Rupert Graves, Amelia Bullmore and Nicholas Gleaves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Abi remains oblivious to Kevin's fate, Tyrone's recent blunder causes heartache at Number 9, and Johnny receives a diagnosis for his condition

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Robin gets the chance to play for his college football team - but a cold threatens to earn him an early bath. Flat-share comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. This time, the hotel hosts a series of BAFTA-related events, which sees a multitude of celebrities and film stars from across the globe coming in and out of the hotel. Head butler Sean shows newest butler Michael the ropes about discretion and how to look after the hotel's most important guests

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: First episode of the drama based on CS Forester's tales of an 18th-century seafaring adventurer. The 17-year-old Horatio Hornblower experiences seasickness while still in Spithead as he begins his Navy career and runs into trouble with a tormentor - until war with France gives him a chance to prove himself in the heat of battle. Ioan Gruffudd and Robert Lindsay star

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team look ahead to another top-class afternoon of racing headlined by the Grade 1 Betfair Chase at Ascot

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents action from October 1979, including Tottenham Hotspur v Nottingham Forest, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa and Brighton & Hove Albion v Norwich City

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Cameroon v England. Gabriel Clarke introduces highlights of the thrilling 1990 World Cup quarter-final from Stadio San Paolo in Naples, Italy. With original coverage hosted by Nick Owen and Elton Welsby and pre-match analysis from Bobby Moore and Jimmy Greaves

programme.title: The Last Sunset (1961)
programme.desc: A wanted murderer travels to Mexico to see the now-married love of his life, little realising he is being pursued by a determined sheriff. However, both the outlaw and the lawman end up working for the woman's husband on a cattle drive. Western, starring Rock Hudson, Kirk Douglas, Dorothy Malone and Joseph Cotten

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training (1986)

Comedy sequel, starring Steve Guttenberg, Bubba Smith, Michael Winslow and Art Metrano

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training (1986)
programme.desc: Facing closure from cutbacks in public spending and competition from a more successful academy, the police training school calls on its bungling graduates to act as instructors and make good officers out of a bunch of new recruits even more incompetent than they are. Comedy sequel, starring Steve Guttenberg, Bubba Smith, Michael Winslow and Art Metrano

programme.title: Demolition Man (1993)
programme.desc: Maverick cop John Spartan is frozen alive in 1996 and held in cryonic suspension for a crime he did not commit. Sci-fi thriller, with Sylvester Stallone, Wesley Snipes, Sandra Bullock and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A new recruit cannot cope with the stresses of the job and puts the lives of other squad members in jeopardy. A young Lesley Dunlop (Emmerdale's Brenda Walker) features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey return with the competitive angling show, with the first edition pitting comedian Bobby Davro against actor Ricky Groves. They head to the tranquillity of a fishing complex in Surrey, where they target crucians, bream, tench and carp

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Tensions hit boiling point on the William Norling as deckhand Macca makes a monumental blunder that threatens to sink the career of Skipper Danny. Meanwhile, Tasmanian skipper Bryce goes head-to-head with a ferocious storm alone as his deckhand Lachy is sick in bed with a stomach bug.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Three bodies from Peru, Colombia and Brazil have been recovered with mysterious injuries that look like burn marks, but who or what could have inflicted them? In an attempt to uncover the perpetrator, Jeremy Wade travels further into the Amazon jungle than ever before, enlisting the help of a secretive and little-known tribe called the Matis to shed light on this unusual case

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: Great moments from the sporting archives.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of races from Newbury, at 2.25, 3.00 and 3.35, as well as two races from Market Rasen, including one at at 2.05. With analysis from AP McCoy and Luke Harvey, reports by Alice Plunkett, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: The Last Sunset (1961)
programme.desc: A wanted murderer travels to Mexico to see the now-married love of his life, little realising he is being pursued by a determined sheriff. However, both the outlaw and the lawman end up working for the woman's husband on a cattle drive. Western, starring Rock Hudson, Kirk Douglas, Dorothy Malone and Joseph Cotten

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman asks Holmes to search for her missing husband, a businessman who was last seen peering from the window of an opium den in London's notorious Limehouse area. Suspicion falls on a well-known local beggar - but his innocence is eventually proved in bizarre fashion. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A home for retired clowns is not all smiles and cream pies as Steed and Tara King look into the killing of the Directors of the Capitol Land and Development Company. John Cleese and Bernard Cribbins guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur buys a hot-air balloon and attempts to break into aerial advertising. However, he is later abducted by a psychotic business rival who takes him to a disused power station, leaving Ray with the puzzle of how to track him down. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie works alongside an old friend who once saved his life while serving in the SAS, and has difficulty coping with mounting evidence that his ex-colleague has joined the gang of Italian terrorists they are shadowing. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An intruder begins breaking into people's homes and destroying their plaster statuettes of Napoleon, but leaving more valuable items untouched. Holmes resolves to find out why, eventually uncovering a twisted tale of theft and murder. Jeremy Brett stars as the master detective

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar visits Venice to save a damsel in distress - only to find himself plunged headlong into a seedy underworld of gangsters, intrigue and deadly revenge. Roger Moore plays the suave secret agent, with William Sylvester, Patrick Troughton and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A missing small-time crook turns up dead, leading to a murder investigation - but Carter's girlfriend develops a peculiar interest in the proceedings. Geraldine James guest stars, with Dennis Waterman and John Thaw

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed suspects that visitor Colonel Nsonga has stolen 3,000 rifles for a revolution in his country. A mercenary called Adriana is behind it, with the aim of selling them to the highest bidder. However, Tara ends up being used for target practice

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes a rebel without a clue by launching a motorbike despatch agency - but Ray is concerned the entire venture is heading for a crash. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle infiltrate a gang smuggling art treasures and industrial secrets out of the country and selling them on to foreign powers. As the investigation proceeds, the duo discover the illicit operation is being run by a country squire in his stately home. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day one of the ranking tournament, featuring two first-round matches, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. The top 16 snooker players on the one-year ranking list are set to feature and the list is headed by world number one Judd Trump, who defends the title he won so convincingly last season, beating current Masters champion Yan Bingtao 10-4 in the final.Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Rush Hour 2 (2001)
programme.desc: The two mismatched detectives travel to Hong Kong when terrorists bomb the US embassy, and are soon involved in keeping tabs on the prime suspects - high-ranking Triad members involved in an international counterfeiting ring. Action comedy sequel, starring Jackie Chan, Chris Tucker, John Lone, Zhang Ziyi, Roselyn Sanchez and Harris Yulin

programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen

programme.title: The Sweeney

However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers

programme.desc: The trainee crew members of the starship Enterprise embark on their maiden flight, which pits them against a Romulan commander from the future who is planning a horrific revenge on those he blames for a tragedy in his own time. Sci-fi adventure based on the original TV series, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto, Eric Bana, Zoe Saldana and Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout (2018)
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and the IMF team join forces with CIA assassin August Walker to prevent a disaster caused by mysterious arms dealer John Lark and a group of terrorists known as the Apostles. However, Hunt and his colleagues are soon pushed to their limits thanks to a face from their past. Writer/director Christopher McQuarrie's sixth entry in the action thriller saga, starring Tom Cruise and Henry Cavill

programme.title: The Last Witch Hunter (2015)
programme.desc: A medieval warrior thwarts an evil witch queen's plan to wipe out the human race, but is cursed with immortality as a result. In the modern day, he continues to battle evil, and faces his greatest challenge when the sorceress is resurrected to resume her evil plan. Fantasy adventure, starring Vin Diesel, Elijah Wood, Rose Leslie and Michael Caine

programme.desc: Socially distanced trio Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by guests including comedian Sophie Duker for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's in Plainfield, New Jersey, but struggles to get his message across to indomitable owner Shelley. Her refusal to deal with the truth comes to a head when Gordon finds a dead mouse in the doorway, prompting him to step up his efforts to change her ways

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Omnibus. Diane finds out who the father of her baby is, and Goldie wonders what could be wrong with Mercedes. Elsewhere, Mandy plans a trip for her and Ella

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and the team make over Curt and Nicola's living room, hall and bedroom in Southbourne, for next to nothing

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The Barones are forced to stay with Ray's parents while their home is fumigated, providing Debra with the perfect opportunity to turn the tables by interfering in Frank and Marie's lives. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank backs into Robert's patrol car, prompting Debra to ban him from driving the kids anywhere - and leaving Ray contemplating his dad's advancing years. American comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny he loves her, but fails to get the response he would have liked. Sheldon also finds himself competing against Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Wil Wheaton once again when the gang goes bowling - and tries to get his revenge

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to hide the fact he had dinner with Penny after she broke up with Leonard, hoping to stay friends with her. However, keeping secrets does not prove to be his strong point, and it isn't long before his deceit is exposed. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon invites a famous physicist to the flat after befriending her online, but is put out to find Leonard showing an interest in her. Meanwhile, Penny has to confront her ex-boyfriend when the visitor turns up in his bedroom. Guest starring Judy Greer (Glenn Martin, DDS)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Depressed at getting old, Bart wears a T-shirt to school bearing an adult joke, and is accosted by a marketing manager wanting to promote the new product. Meanwhile, Homer helps Lisa build a nuclear bomb for a school science project. Eric Idle guest stars

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Hard-hearted Mr Burns decides to deny his employees any more free medication, prompting Homer to whisk Grampa away on a drug-smuggling mission to Canada. Unfortunately, the duo fall foul of the authorities on their return journey

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer teaches victory dance moves to famous athletes, and proves so successful that he is chosen to produce the Super Bowl half-time show. Meanwhile, Ned gets backing from Mr Burns to create his own versions of Bible stories, but they are far from popular. With the voice of figure skater Michelle Kwan

programme.title: Sunday Brunch

programme.title: A Taste of Italy
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur Nisha Katona embarks on a less-travelled path to reveal Italy's hidden foodie gems, beginning with a look at Orvieto in the Umbria mountains

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart disobeys Marge's wishes and attends a rap concert, where he ends up performing with the stars on stage. While returning home, he realises how much trouble he will be in - so he decides to fake his own kidnapping, leading to the arrest of Milhouse's dad. With the guest voice of 50 Cent

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A hillbilly's tirade about Springfield prompts Mayor Quimby to legalise same-sex marriage in the town. Homer sees the opportunity to cash in by getting ordained over the internet and conducting weddings himself. As scores of people come out of the closet, Marge's sister Patty makes a shock announcement

programme.title: Puss in Boots (2011)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giant's castle. He must save the day when it transpires that their actions have unleashed a terrible danger on his home town. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek

programme.title: Ice Age: Collision Course (2016)
programme.desc: When a cosmic blunder leaves Earth in the path of an asteroid, it's up to Sid the sloth and pals Manny and Diego to save the day. Animated sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, Denis Leary, John Leguizamo and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Isle of Dogs (2018)
programme.desc: As a nasty and extremely virulent canine flu ravages the technologically advanced metropolis of Megasaki, Japan, the unscrupulous and corrupt mayor decrees that all dogs be banished to Trash Island - a vast offshore wasteland of refuse. The mayor's 12-year-old orphaned nephew Atari summons the courage to set off alone on a dangerous rescue mission to track down his beloved four-legged companion. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Bryan Cranston, Koyu Rankin and Edward Norton

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down

programme.desc: A chance to catch-up with the best moments from last year's episodes, featuring some of the country's favourite celebrities. The shows featured are This Morning, Planet Earth II, Big Brother: Best Shows Ever, Grease, Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back, World Cup Final '66, Dating Around and Misery

programme.title: Ted 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The foul-mouthed talking teddy bear is horrified to discover that he counts as property according to the law. He and his human best friend hire a pot-smoking lawyer to help them fight a court case and get Ted legally recognised as a person. Comedy sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg and Amanda Seyfried, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home

programme.desc: Sam asks Carla to help him exact revenge on John Allen Hill, the irritating owner of the seafood restaurant above Cheers, but she ends up falling for him instead. programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.desc: Martin and Frasier inadvertently offer to pay for Daphne's wedding, while Niles sinks into gloom as the marriage approaches and tries to console himself by dating someone new - unaware she is a prostitute. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth to host in Surrey is 56-year-old music teacher Caroline, who hopes an evening of singing, dancing and nursery rhymes will increase her chances of bagging the money

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Uber-competitive businessman John is banking on his home-cooked menu to bag him the prize at the end of the night. However, John's one-upmanship and lengthy anecdotes have annoyed hairdresser Ben, who feels that he would be the smuggest contestant ever if he wins

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.desc: Mother-of-three Sheila wants a family friendly property in the thriving resort of Benalmadena on the Costa del Sol, with its sunny beaches and lively atmosphere. With a budget of £135,000 and specifying the need for sea views, Danni Menzies sets out to deliver the dream place

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Catching up with more British expats who set up new businesses abroad, revisiting a luxury B&B in Spain as its owners launch a new sideline they hope will turn a profit

programme.title: The Simpsons
